question: What must all remote work be conducted over when accessing internal systems?
expected_answer: All remote work must be conducted over TechCorp's VPN when accessing internal systems.
chunk_text: All remote work must be conducted over TechCorp's VPN when accessing internal systems.

question: Within how many days must business expenses be submitted through the expense management system?
expected_answer: All business expenses must be submitted within 30 days of the expense being incurred.
chunk_text: All business expenses must be submitted through the expense management system within 30 days of the expense being incurred.

question: How much notice must an employee provide in writing before resigning?
expected_answer: Employees must provide at least two weeks written notice to their manager before resigning.
chunk_text: If you decide to leave TechCorp, please provide at least two weeks written notice to your manager.

question: What is the email address employees should contact if their device is lost or stolen?
expected_answer: If a device is lost or stolen, employees must report it immediately to the IT Security team at security@techcorp.com.
chunk_text: If your device is lost or stolen, report it to the IT Security team immediately at security@techcorp.com.

question: What is the home office stipend amount provided to new employees?
expected_answer: TechCorp provides a home office stipend of $1,500 USD to all new employees, to be used within the first 90 days of employment.
chunk_text: TechCorp provides a home office stipend of $1,500 USD to all new employees, to be used within the first 90 days of employment.

question: How long is the vesting period for stock options at TechCorp?
expected_answer: Stock options vest over a four-year period, with a one-year cliff. This means 25% of options vest after the first year, and the remainder vest monthly over the following three years.
chunk_text: Options vest over a four-year period, with a one-year cliff. This means that 25% of your options vest after your first year, and the remainder vest monthly over the following three years.

question: How many days of paid bereavement leave does TechCorp provide?
expected_answer: TechCorp provides three days of paid bereavement leave following the death of an immediate family member.
chunk_text: TechCorp provides three days of paid bereavement leave following the death of an immediate family member, defined as a spouse, partner, child, parent, or sibling.

question: What is the annual professional development budget per employee?
expected_answer: TechCorp offers a professional development budget of $2,000 USD per employee per year, which can be used for conferences, courses, books, certifications, or any other learning activity relevant to the role.
chunk_text: TechCorp also offers a professional development budget of $2,000 USD per employee per year.

question: When can an employee begin taking parental leave after a child is born?
expected_answer: Parental leave can be taken at any point within the first 12 months following the birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child.
chunk_text: Leave can be taken at any point within the first 12 months following the birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child.

question: What happens to unexercised stock options after an employee leaves TechCorp?
expected_answer: After leaving TechCorp, employees have 90 days to exercise their vested options. After 90 days, unexercised options expire.
chunk_text: You will have 90 days following your last day of employment to exercise any vested options. After 90 days, unexercised options expire.

question: How long do employees have to use the new hire workspace setup allowance?
expected_answer: The home office stipend of $1,500 USD must be used within the first 90 days of employment.
chunk_text: TechCorp provides a home office stipend of $1,500 USD to all new employees, to be used within the first 90 days of employment.

question: What is the minimum recommended amount of time off employees should take each year?
expected_answer: Employees are encouraged to take at least 20 days off per year.
chunk_text: Employees are encouraged to take at least 20 days off per year. Taking time off is not optional — rest is considered essential to sustainable performance.

question: How long after leaving the company can employees act on their vested equity?
expected_answer: Employees have 90 days following their last day of employment to exercise any vested options. After 90 days, unexercised options expire.
chunk_text: You will have 90 days following your last day of employment to exercise any vested options. After 90 days, unexercised options expire.

question: What connectivity requirement must remote employees meet to work from home?
expected_answer: Remote employees must have a reliable internet connection capable of supporting video calls and large file transfers.
chunk_text: You must have a reliable internet connection capable of supporting video calls and large file transfers.

question: What must employees do before spending from their learning and growth budget?
expected_answer: Employees must obtain approval from their manager before spending from the professional development budget.
chunk_text: Approval from your manager is required before spending from this budget.

question: What is the minimum expense amount that requires a receipt for reimbursement?
expected_answer: Receipts are required for all expenses above $25.
chunk_text: Receipts are required for all expenses above $25. Expenses submitted without receipts will not be reimbursed.

question: Which teams may have different in-office expectations compared to most employees?
expected_answer: Certain roles — primarily those in Sales, Customer Success, and Executive Leadership — may have different in-office expectations defined in their employment contracts.
chunk_text: Certain roles — primarily those in Sales, Customer Success, and Executive Leadership — may have different in-office expectations defined in their employment contracts.

question: What is the health benefit provided to TechCorp employees based outside the United States?
expected_answer: Employees outside the United States receive a monthly health stipend of $300 USD to be used toward private health insurance or other health-related expenses.
chunk_text: For employees outside the United States, TechCorp provides a monthly health stipend of $300 USD to be used toward private health insurance or other health-related expenses.

question: How frequently must managers and employees meet one-on-one at a minimum?
expected_answer: Employees and managers are expected to meet one-on-one at least twice per month.
chunk_text: Employees and managers are expected to meet one-on-one at least twice per month.

question: When do salary adjustments from the annual review take effect?
expected_answer: Salary adjustments resulting from the annual review take effect on February 1st of each year.
chunk_text: Salary adjustments resulting from reviews take effect on February 1st of each year.

question: What happens to an employee's stock options both during and after their employment ends at TechCorp?
expected_answer: Unvested options are forfeited when an employee leaves. Employees have 90 days after their last day to exercise vested options, after which unexercised options expire.
chunk_text: Chunk 13: 'If you leave TechCorp before your options have fully vested, you forfeit the unvested portion. You will have 90 days following your last day of employment to exercise any vested options.' Chunk 23: offboarding session, final paycheck, vested equity info.

question: What security-related obligations must a remote employee fulfill when working from home?
expected_answer: Remote employees must use TechCorp's VPN when accessing internal systems, ensure their home network is secure, and use a TechCorp-issued laptop for work involving internal systems or customer data.
section: Work Hours and Remote Work + Security and Data Privacy
subsection: 3.2 Remote Work Policy + 7.1 Device and Account Security
chunk_text: Chunk 6: 'All remote work must be conducted over TechCorp's VPN when accessing internal systems.' Chunk 18: 'All employees must use a TechCorp-issued laptop for work that involves accessing internal systems, customer data, or source code.'

question: What are the combined leave entitlements for an employee who is both ill and recently became a parent?
expected_answer: Sick days are unlimited and separate from PTO. Parental leave is 16 weeks of fully paid leave, available at any point within the first 12 months after birth, adoption, or foster placement.
chunk_text: Chunk 9: 'Sick days are separate and unlimited.' Chunk 10: 'TechCorp provides 16 weeks of fully paid parental leave for all new parents.'

question: Can a TechCorp employee choose to work from a co-working space instead of their home?
expected_answer: Yes. TechCorp allows employees to work from any location of their choosing, provided they meet the connectivity, security, and availability requirements.
chunk_text: All employees are allowed to work from home or any location of their choosing, provided they meet the following conditions.

question: Is taking time off considered optional or essential at TechCorp?
expected_answer: Taking time off is not optional at TechCorp. Rest is considered essential to sustainable performance, and employees are encouraged to take at least 20 days off per year.
chunk_text: Taking time off is not optional — rest is considered essential to sustainable performance.

question: Does disclosing a conflict of interest mean an employee has acted wrongfully?
expected_answer: No. Disclosing a conflict of interest does not automatically mean the employee has done something wrong. Many conflicts can be managed appropriately once disclosed.
chunk_text: Disclosing a conflict of interest does not automatically mean you have done something wrong. Many conflicts can be managed appropriately once they are disclosed.

question: In what situation is an employee not required to submit a doctor's note for sick leave?
expected_answer: Employees are not required to provide a doctor's note for absences of fewer than five consecutive days.
chunk_text: You are not required to provide a doctor's note for absences of fewer than five consecutive days.

question: What information will the IT team never ask an employee to provide?
expected_answer: The IT team will never ask for an employee's password. Employees must not share their credentials with anyone, including members of the IT team.
chunk_text: Do not share your credentials with anyone, including members of the IT team. The IT team will never ask for your password.

question: How much money does TechCorp allocate per employee annually for career-related growth?
expected_answer: TechCorp provides a professional development budget of $2,000 USD per employee per year, which can be used for conferences, courses, books, certifications, or other relevant learning activities.
chunk_text: TechCorp also offers a professional development budget of $2,000 USD per employee per year.

question: How often does TechCorp conduct its compensation-related review cycle?
expected_answer: TechCorp conducts salary reviews annually, in January. Salary adjustments take effect on February 1st of each year.
chunk_text: TechCorp conducts salary reviews annually, in January. Salary adjustments resulting from reviews take effect on February 1st of each year.

question: Is there any official guarantee against punishment for staff members who raise concerns about workplace wrongdoing?
expected_answer: Yes. Retaliation against anyone who reports harassment in good faith is strictly prohibited and is itself a violation of TechCorp's policy.
chunk_text: Retaliation against anyone who reports harassment in good faith is strictly prohibited and is itself a violation of this policy.
vocab_gap_note: Question uses: guarantee/punishment/staff members/raise concerns/workplace wrongdoing. Chunk uses: strictly prohibited/anyone/reports harassment/good faith. Zero direct lexical overlap.

question: Above what spending level must an employee obtain clearance from their direct superior before committing to the outlay?
expected_answer: Expenses above $500 require pre-approval from the employee's manager before they are incurred.
chunk_text: Expenses above $500 require pre-approval from your manager before they are incurred.
vocab_gap_note: Question uses: spending level/obtain clearance/direct superior/committing/outlay. Chunk uses: expenses/$500/pre-approval/manager/incurred. Zero direct lexical overlap.

question: What notice period or severance pay does TechCorp provide when it decides to let an employee go?
expected_answer: The TechCorp handbook does not address company-initiated termination, severance pay, or any employer-side notice period. Section 9 covers only voluntary resignation and the offboarding process for employee-initiated departures.
chunk_text: Section 9 covers voluntary resignation (9.1) and offboarding (9.2) only. Company-initiated termination, severance pay, and employer-side notice periods are not addressed anywhere in this document. A correct RAG response must state that this information is not available.
precision_note: Dense retrieval will likely surface chunks 22 (Resignation) and 23 (Offboarding) with moderate cosine scores (~0.5-0.6). The test measures whether the pipeline avoids a false positive — these adjacent chunks do not answer the question.

question: Does TechCorp offer an annual performance bonus or any other variable pay, and if so, how is the amount determined?
expected_answer: The TechCorp handbook does not describe any performance bonus or variable compensation. Section 5 covers salary, equity options, health benefits, and additional stipends, but no bonus structure is mentioned anywhere in the document.
chunk_text: Section 5 covers: 5.1 Salary (annual review, market data, performance — no bonus), 5.2 Equity (stock options), 5.3 Health Benefits, 5.4 Additional Benefits (stipends). No performance bonus or variable pay policy exists in this document. A correct RAG response must state that this information is not available.
precision_note: Dense retrieval will likely surface chunk 12 (Salary, mentions 'individual performance') and chunk 13 (Equity) as false positives. The word 'performance' also appears in chunk 16 (Performance Reviews). Test measures whether these adjacent chunks trigger a confident but incorrect answer.
